Het probleem blijft, ik heb voorgesteld om deze lift terug naar DeVi te sturen ter inspectie. Gaat samen met lift UP-005754.

Findings Jorrit, 
-traction axle bent, replaced 
-traction wheel replaced due to new axle 
- roller wheel replaced due to new axle 
-guidance covers replaced due to switches from mounting brackets
 -Osg right with cord replaced. (Forgot to scan, don't know which number went in.) 
-software update from 2.4.8.3 to 2.4.8.5 -holiday switch installed incl cables etc. 
-the main print was quite abused!! Connectors 7 and 9 had to be reattached to the print by Henk because they were almost pulled off the print. Plug 4 got a new plug because 1 of the wires was pulled loose. Plug 7 was replaced because 1 pole had turned black. Petra replaced the power cables on the bottom of the print because they were damaged to such an extent that the copper was visible. 
-traction motor checked and adjusted. 
-batteries were both healthy and put back in.

All parts are on the expense of DeVi. The repair time can be invoiced to 24-7.

DescriptionMalfunction:
- problemen:

Helaas had ik een paar problemen met UP-005479: de aandrijving werd verschillende keren onderbroken door veiligheidspictogram, time-out en draaikoppeling niet in neutrale positie. Zie screenshot.

Ik heb toen de zitting vervangen. Maar dat hielp niet, de fouten waren er nog steeds. Toen heb ik de hoofdprint vervangen. De nieuwe hoofdprintplaat laadde echter niet op bij de stoppen. Ik heb toen een derde hoofdprint geïnstalleerd. De verbinding met de printplaat van de stoel kon echter niet tot stand worden gebracht. Dus verving ik de stoelprintplaat door de originele stoelprintplaat. Toen traden de fouten weer op. Dus verving ik de stoelprintplaat opnieuw door een derde stoelprintplaat.

Toen werkte alles 5 minuten en plotseling waren daar weer dezelfde fouten als in het begin. Het systeem heeft nog steeds dezelfde fouten. We hebben de volgende printen:

- Hoofdprint 1 - met fouten zoals in de schermafbeelding
- Hoofdprintplaat 2 - laad de accus niet op de laadpunten (is een print uit de sparepart kit)
- Hoofdprintplaat 3 - met fouten zoals in de schermafbeelding
- Printplaat 1 van de stoel - met fouten zoals in de schermafbeelding
- Seat PCB 2 - geen verbinding mogelijk
- Seat PCB 3 - met fouten zoals in het screenshot
  Het systeem draait momenteel niet betrouwbaar en blijft crashen. Ik weet niet wat er aan de hand is, het draait al ongeveer 2 maanden, de fouten zijn er al ongeveer 1 week.
CauseMalfunction: Unit werkt bij mij goed. Tractieas is krom en heb ik vervangen. Heeft een nacht lang heen en weer gereden zonder problemen.
Solution: Spline axle vervangen ivm krom. Laadpunt wat hoger gemaakt voor minder frictie tussen laadpunt en stoel. Software update van 2.4.8.3 naar 2.4.8.5. Unit is in bekisting geplaatst waarin het is ontvangen en met testrapport toegevoegd aan tweedehands in hal 2

DescriptionMalfunction:
Leider mussten wir heute auch bei UP-005146 das Ladegerät tauschen.
LED blinkt abwechselnd schnell rot / grün und ladet nicht.
Part 2 (17-3-2025): E21 shows and going down, the spindle accelerates to get the seat tilted. This is an indication of a faulty encoder signal. 
The power wires are also melted partially, indicating a high current. In the registration report I can see a peak current of 17000mA, which could indicate that the rail configuration was not completely correct and the seat tilting away from the staircase, causing extra strain on the spindle.


The spindle motor on this lift is defective.
As soon as a level change occurs, the chair jerks to one side. Error E 21 is displayed.
The cable from the spindle motor to the main circuit board was melted.
Apparently the current consumption of the spindle motor is too high.

- The cable/insulation was repaired and the cable checked/measured. This is now OK.
- After the error still occurred, the main circuit board was replaced.
The fault still exists.
All other possible causes of the fault have been ruled out. The complete wiring harness was also checked.

The only remaining cause is the spindle motor.

Can you please send us a new drive unit / carriage for this lift urgently?
And not just a spindle motor, please. I don't want us to spend hours dismantling the travelling unit so that we can replace the motor.

My patience and that of our customers is slowly coming to an end with the UP lifts.
There is virtually no UP that runs faultlessly. The costs for the numerous warranty calls (apart from the material) remain with us.

I've been in the industry for 15 years, so I have a lot of comparisons with other products. And we also have other products in our range that work faultlessly.

Please send a new one as soon as possible. The customer is dependent on the lift.

Please find attached a video and photos of this case.
Solution:
Dealer heeft een lader uit eigen voorraad gebruikt.

week 17 is de carriage ontvangen.

DescriptionMalfunction:
The lift continues driving without joystick input. 
The lift sometimes does not stop on a middle station, even though the user is not using the joystick and no remotes are installed.
There is proof from the cctv camera. While dismounting, the lift starts driving again and the user presses the joystick to the other side to stop the lift. However afterwards, the lift starts driving again.
Deel 2: E6 error
Part 3 (19-2-2025): The technician has replaced the batteries because of the low supply voltage warning. The system current peak is 50A and the temperature goes up to 62 degrees. Traction motor pulls 27A when on an horizontal bend, but only going upwards. Technician can hear a screaching noise from the lift (brake).

Remco 24-3-2025: Paras has received the brake and encoder replacement set and has succesfully replaced it. The brake is audibly clicking now. Temperature around 40 degrees in the system. Encoder values are correct. Batteries seem to be a bit low (23.4V off charging contact), although Paras said he just replaced them. After replacement, the lift drives up but not down and gives E6. Can't see a traction motor current. The connector 7 (traction motor power) was replaced some time with another connector and cable. The female connector on the pcb is broken off, although the pins are still intact. The terminals from the traction motor are not good and need replacement.

Remco 11-4-2025, message from Zafar: We are currently at site UP-1855 to resolve the ongoing issues. At this site, The PCB was found to be faulty, and the old motor was not working properly (motor winding data: old – 0.7, new – 1.8 in continuity mode). We replaced both the PCB (provided by Shuritika Ma'am) and the motor.The chair is now working. However, we have suggested rectifying the rail alignment for better performance.
CauseMalfunction:
Either the joystick or the chair pcb are faulty.

Part 3: I think the traction motor brake is failing. This can cause the high current, the high temperature and also the noise.

Remco 24-3-2025: Most likely the traction motor power terminals have bad connection. Why the lift drives up but not down, I don't know.
Solution:
Paras replaced the joystick, I asked to confirm if this has solved the issue.

Part 3: check the brake and see if it is stuck. Try to clean the brake and see if it releases again. Otherwise, send replacement traction motor.

Remco 24-3-2025: replace the traction motor connector and terminals to start with. The main pcb is also damaged and might need replacement.

Jorrit: 13/5/2025 received  Two mainprint sets, 1 tractionmotor, 1 tractionmotor rem. 

The traction motor is faulty indeed. The brake was a testing part and does not need to be credited.

DescriptionMalfunction: Tech reported this has been installed for about a month working great till yesterday. Lift was stuck in a 45 degree down bend with 5 degree error. We checked all rail data, checked rail angles, reset lift, zero’d out encoder to 0 at top level overrun just to be sure, checked charge station distance, everything checked out. Running the lift down the unit would keep stopping randomly getting Traction motor illegal transitions, we inspected plug #8 and wiring all looks good. After a handful of these errors in a row it seemed to not want to spindle in the correct location. We got unit back up to top using override, re centered and got lift to run all the way down without any errors, on the way up the lift started stopping getting the Traction encoder Illegal Transitions again, we would have to clear the error and continue to run, the lift then wasn’t spindling correctly in the Bends again after this error happened 3-4 times stopping the lift on the way up
CauseMalfunction: 3 options: Traction motor encoder pins are not making proper contact, wire broken, cable mantle touching the brake and causing interference.
Solution: I suggested to slightly bend the connector terminals inwards and see if that changes anything. Otherwise, the traction motor needs replacement.

DescriptionMalfunction:
Brandon: Investigation: Missed service report from 3/5/25. Dealer called in with hinge rail failure, could hear motor clicking but no movement, same thing when tried with 9V battery, loosed up support on rail to make sure it wasn’t pinching the motor.

Part 2 (replacing the original hingerail): 
Investigation: Today tech received new hingerail got it installed, we ran lift all the way to bottom, on the way up we stopped on hinge station and programmed, and hingerail folded up as it should. We then ran lift up to charge contact, went back down to make sure hinge would go down. We saw no movement in down direction at all, no sounds, no movement. We made sure good contact of charge strips, watching statistics Live MA stayed at 0 never moved. We then tested the hinge with 9v battery and it did go down. We then loosened up screws on the motor plate on the hingerail to slide it one way or another, did not take the screws all the way out just loosened up 1 turn. Tightened screws back up and now get no movement or sounds from hingerail at all. Tech went and got a new 9v battery and still nothing. Dealer is starting to get upset and losing trust in Hingerails.

Resolution: Dealer is looking at option on putting a drop nose in instead of another warranty Hingerail, he is talking it over with customer and deciding. I will keep you informed with what they are wanting to do.

 This is the 2nd Hingerail, this one being new right out of box that doesn’t work.
CauseMalfunction:
I am assuming both hingerails are faulty, but I would like to have the main pcb checked too. 

The second hingerail was unfolding initially with 9V battery, but after adjusting not anymore. 0 mA in the beginning could point to a fault in the spindle station configuration, the motor assembly, the cable or the main pcb hingerail driver.
Solution:
Loosen the motor assembly screws one turn, and try folding up/down with the 9V battery and then with the lift. If that works, there is a problem with the placement of the motor assembly. 
If that does not work, investigate the results and determine if the hingerail or the main pcb needs replacement.
JORRIT: received hingerail 23-6-2025.
Hingerail Motor runs heavy 1.3Ah, I'm going to check. 8x screw hinge tightened. Unfortunately, falling off the table became unusable.

DescriptionMalfunction:
The seat is going out of level, even when the lift is stopped. 

We tried a new motherboard, but the problem came back.
Sometimes we can provoke the problem:
On a steep place with a 60° angle of inclination, you lean to the right and then to the left while driving and suddenly the whole chair tilts to the side. Even if you let go of the joystick (as shown in the video).
It is as if the spindle motor can run empty and has no resistance.
The wiring is fine and the batteries too.
No errors are displayed. The usual errors (5° to the side and so on) are only displayed when the chair is at an angle.

I understand very well that you want to investigate what is going on here.
But the customer urgently needs the lift after a stroke and we do not have the extra resources to make multiple trips to the customer until we have a solution.
And we have to bear the many costs that arise from the warranty repairs to the UP lifts.

The customer is already very irritated. He has already received a new trolley once because the drive motor had too much play and unfortunately we are on site every 4 - 8 weeks because something is not working (no user error).
Last Friday I was on site to repair the charging cable in the lift because it had broken off at the connection to the contacts (the trolley is only 1 year old!).
Solution:
Send new carriage,

DescriptionMalfunction:
Hingerail not folding. 
Created a report on this lift, but the hingerail is on stock and not installed at this lift.
Remco 14-5-2025: The new hingerail is unfolding but does not fold. No current is seen when trying to fold up. Older lifts from 2021 and 2022 are working with this hingerail, the lifts from 2023 and 2025 are not.  L5 is cracked on all of the boards. 
-The 2025 lifts are UP-006230, UP-006246 which tested and failed on this hinge rail at the client. 
-Replaced the hinge rail for a new one at the client and with older lift, now working fine
-Tested a replacement board from service kit in UP-006230 on a newly sent hinge rail, board failed failed
-The second of two replacement hingerails are working and installed at client together with older lift. They checked all 3 hingerails in stock and none of them have any shorts now.
Remco 21-5-2025: The new charging contact with internal connecten from negative to the rail were used for the hingerail. This shorted the wires and blew up the main boards.
CauseMalfunction:
Hingerail overcurrent. 
Remco 14-5-2025: The original hingerail is faulty for sure. 3 main pcb's are also faulty, 2 of which for sure because of the hingerail.
The third board was in UP-006230, it could be that this has a short in the wiring because of the earlier failure.
Solution:
Send replacement hingerail. 
Remco 14-5-2025: The new hingerail is working. 
Send 2x replacement main pcb for this lift. UP-006246 is already receiving a main pcb.
The latest order was sent with warranty, but the hinge was shorted by changing the contacts. No warranty.

DescriptionMalfunction:
The OSG is activating. The OSG was replaced some 3 months ago with a new one (ordered around new year so it's a good one). 
Without weight, the lift is driving okay. With a person on it, it is activating every time driving down. 
I can see the OSG being pressed up and down into the rail, this is a sing of a torted traction motor axle. 
The traction wheel itself has dents in the side from driving over gearrack screws. 
When the lift is stopped on E3, the OSG itself did not dig into the rail, there are no marks.
Remco 28-5-2025: The main pcb was replaced because the footrest was not going up (client was pushing it up). 
The OSG is now activating when going up, but only when no client is using it.
CauseMalfunction:
Seems like the OSG is wedged in some way, causing friction and the OSG to activate. 
Since they replaced the OSG recently, I am not sure if the installation was done entirely correct. 
The torted axle and damaged pinion wheel can have an effect, but not certain if this is really the cause.
Solution:
Take off the lift to properly inspect the OSG and other internals. 
-Is the OSG moving freely? 
-Is the spring connected properly? 
-Is the switch installed properly? 
-Inspect the pinion wheel

If all is good, try adding another spring to pull back the OSG with more force.
Remco 12-8-2025: have not heard back about this lift, I am closing the SR

DescriptionMalfunction:
The pinion wheel is excessively worn, and there are scratch marks on the rail at the end of the horizontal bend due to the OSG nylon wheel wall being pushed into the rail wall.
Remco 2-7-2025: Francisco replaced the pinion wheel, OSG and gearrack, but still experiencing the same issue. The OSG roller is touching the outside wall in horizontal bends, causing damage to the roller and rail. The gearrack is properly lubricated this time.
CauseMalfunction:
After better inspection, I noticed that the gearrack was not lubricated. This has caused the excessive wear on the traction wheel and gearrack.
Remco0 2-7-2025: The traction motor axle is not guided properly by the gearrack, or it is not sliding in and out properly.
Solution:
Replace the traction wheel, gearrack and possibly the OSG. The OSG might be okay still, but that will become clear upon inspection. If the guidance walls are okay, then it can be repurposed. 
Also make lubricate the gearrack and traction axle.
Remco 2-7-2025: I advised to place the gearrack as much to the inside as possible and also lubricate the traction motor axle in the part where it slides in and out of the traction motor.

DescriptionMalfunction:

Remco 18-6-2025: Approval by Dennis to send a new seatbase to fix this lift. The main pcb needs to be updated though.
Send pickit with the lift, as Harmar does not have functioning pickits at the moment. Will be sent to Harmar after the update.

DescriptionMalfunction: Chair is skewed under load without control.
CauseMalfunction: Dealer heeft Interflon fin Grease smeermiddel gebruikt op de spindel. Dealer has used Interflon fin Grease lubricant on the spindle.
Solution:
I took the spindle out of the carriage and cleaned it with Loctite SF 7063. The bronze block and safety nut have also been cleaned with Loctite SF 7063. The spindle is greased with EM-30 L grease and placed back in the carriage. The Charging Terminal Carriage Assembly has been replaced because it damaged charging contacts. The mainprint has been updated to V2.4.9.1. The carriage has been tested on the test track and has done so with good results. The carriage is provided with a test report, cleaned, placed in a wooden formwork and added to second-hand. The seat is equipped with a new belt cassette due to old model. The seat is added separately from the carriage for second-hand.
EXTRA OPMERKING: de spindel is uitvoerig getest met diverse smeermiddelen te weten: EM-30 L, siliconen spray, WD40, gearrack smeermiddel. Uit de smeermiddelen test is gebleken dat de spindel zelfremmend is, en dus voldoet. De mainprint en spindelmotor zijn uitvoerig getest en onderzocht door Liam en Michael en goed bevonden. eindeconclusie: lift ging scheef door verkeerd smeermiddel dealer.
FinanceComment:
De zitting is bij deze gescheiden van de carriage omdat er vaker vraag is naar een losse carriage ipv een complete lift(Gemeld bij Rox). De gordel cassete had ik nog over, vandaar dat het niet bij producten staat. Ook de houten bekisting had ik nog liggen.

DescriptionMalfunction: Blue and red thread of the spindle engine have melted.
CauseMalfunction: peak current of 17 Ah measured at the spindle motor according to mainprint log. Together with Henk, I have examined and measured the engine and it gives too little resistance. You can brake the engine with your hand by holding the axle.
Solution: I replaced the spindle engine with a new one and thrown away the old one. The bearing lock plate was therefore also curved so this one was also replaced. I completely cleaned the spindle and examined it for any damage. The spindle is still fine. The spindle is greased and replaced. Mainprint update of V2.4.8.3 -> V2.4.9.1. The minus strip of the AB420000 charging terminal Carriage assembly is missing in its entirety. Because this is also the old type of terminal, I replace it in its entirety. The carriage was tested, cleaned, provided with a test report, placed in a wooden formwork that I still had lying around and added to second-hand.

DescriptionMalfunction:
Tech reported unit was at top location, customer never powered it down, unit had EEPROM CRC INCORRECT. Tech reset all motors error went away. Just want to report this error incase it happens again. 
After technician re calibrated motors, he was testing the lift making sure the chair angle was staying good, and at the top halfway through the bend the lift stopped and got spindle encoder failure. The battery Voltage was still at 24.7V, the tech reported a “hot” smell from the lift as well. We proceeded to try and spindle override after clearing the error and we could not get the spindle motor to move at all, it would continue to get spindle encoder failure, we would clear the error and try again. All other override features would work but the spindle motor. We tried the buttons on the board as well and those would not work either. The tech reported the spindle motor was very hot to touch, it seems like the spindle motor is froze up at this point.. I also had technician try applying a little bit of pressure to the chair when trying to spindle and still nothing..
Technician did send support mail from the lift which you should receive when he gets back to office.
On 8/11- the tech was out there for spindle encoder failure, which I helped clear with technician and I had ruled it off as possible low batteries as we couldn’t get error to occur again while tech was there..
On 8/18- Tech was out there again for traction brake failure, that we were able to get to clear as well. As well as comm timed out, wrong logic voltage, angle comparator errors. That we were able to get cleared..
This customer has been decent till now, and is refusing any type of repair on the unit. At this point we have to send the dealer either a Right Hand Carraige (No seat) or a Right Hand Chassis/Seat combined, as they have threatened the dealer with legal action and is refusing a spindle motor replacement.
CauseMalfunction: Issue with the main pcb and a number of other issues.
Solution: We are sending a replacement carriage and inspect the faulty one at Harmar.
